source: ' You are now a stranger to {SettlementName}.
(For buildings of ALL tiers, not only T1)
* We are using generative AI during development to prototype the voice acting. Expect professional voice acting for the full release.

...We're fighting to save these innocents, but it's getting harder by the day.<cf>Who could have known that The Butcher, with that grim name, is someone who's got a soft spot for kids. Together, we're a makeshift family in this mess.<cf>But there's talk of a rat among us. Someone leaking our next moves, maybe even leading the king's dogs to our door. Can't trust anyone these days.<cf>And then there's my own kid. Always looking at me like they know something's up. Makes my skin crawl. I'm in this to protect them, but how do you shield your own from a storm like this?<cf>Every move we make feels like a gamble. But we're in too deep now to turn back...
100 Coins. I don’t care where the money comes from, but I’m not returning empty-handed.
100 coins? That's daylight robbery! The miller's in a bind, and you're exploiting the situation!
2 Agility: Novice Books and 2 Archery: Novice Books
2 Strength: Novice Books and 2 Shields: Novice Books
50 gold coins is not enough. Make it 100, and I'm in.
<Tell Cyrus about Red Thorns attack and mole inside caravan>

<The old man speaks to you in an incomprehensible language. Certain words sound similar to your own native tongue. You can piece together some of the meaning. What caught your attention were phrases instructing you on how to build shrines and prepare some concoction.>
<highlight>Observe</> your enemies carefully when they attack and block in the same direction.<cf><cf>Remember that <highlight>footwork</> and keeping distance are the key to success.
A bag full of axe heads that should be made without the need to vipe out the entire bandit camp.
A baked dish which is usually made of a pastry dough casing that contains a filling of various sweet or savoury ingredients.
A beneficial exchange for both our villages, indeed.
A better version of the simple pickaxe used for mining ores and stones. Can also be used as weapon.
A better version of the simple pickaxe used for mining ores and stones. Can also be used as weapon.
A bit pricey, but we're desperate. Here's the gold.
A boar… A BOAR?! I don’t know what to say, I’m stunned. I hope the Elder doesn’t exile him.
A break… I haven't considered that. It could help clear my head. Thanks for the suggestion.
A bunch of lads that hate the Brigands and Bandits even more than me.
A colored material that is completely or nearly insoluble in water.
A colored material that is completely or nearly insoluble in water.
A common fish that lives in the lakes and rivers of the Lowlands. Often sold by local fishermen. Inedible raw due to a high concentration of parasites that need to be cooked out.
A common herb that can be used to add flavor to dishes or to treat various ailments.
A common herb that can be used to add flavor to dishes or to treat various ailments.
A common herb that can be used to add flavor to dishes or treat various ailments.
A common herb that can be used to add flavor to dishes. Grows mostly in fields. Can be planted on a farm.
A common type of carbonate sedimentary rock. Can be mined from certain rocks. Can be found in the Southern parts of Karvenia.
A couple nights ago, I got a visit from some old mates who had left the Lord’s guard, like I did back in the day. They came over for a drink and a chinwag.

A couple of my friends want to go on a trip outside the Lowlands, you know what I mean right? And I want to keep them safe so let’s say you bring me some reinforced gambesons and sturdy helmets and I will give you some gold for the trouble.
A couple of trade caravans and we could buy food for the next three winters for the whole village.
A cozy building that can house up to 7 settlers and significantly affect their morale.
A crowd gathered, and they were furious. They were all shouting at Dobbins. He had nowhere to run.
A dancing light in the ruins? That's quite the sight.
A durable blend of flax and cotton fibers. Crafted by skilled artisans.
A durable blend of flax and cotton fibers. Crafted by skilled artisans.
A fair response. I understand the challenges. Your efforts, however sporadic, are still very much appreciated. I'll ensure that your findings are put to good use.
A farmer in Padstow needs help with a cow bitten by wolves. He thinks you can whip up a cure. Got a bit of a problem.
A fat bag of coins for your trouble. What do you think?
A few leaves steeped in hot water make a tea that calms the stomach and eases the mind. It's also said to ward off evil spirits, but that's more a matter of belief, I suppose.
A few leaves steeped in hot water make a tea that calms the stomach and eases the mind. It's also said to ward off evil spirits, but that's more a matter of belief, I suppose.
A few leaves steeped in hot water make a tea that calms the stomach and eases the mind. It's also said to ward off evil spirits, but that's more a matter of belief, I suppose.
A flowering plant that has fibers that can be used for many things. Native to the Lowlands. Grows mostly in fields. Can be planted on a farm.
A former bandit. Don’t worry, he won’t bother Haerndean anymore.
A friend of Oscar Lanslet. I came to seek your wisdom.
A fully fiber that can be woven into fabric. Raw cotton can be harvested wild in the Highlands.
A good plan. We'll train and equip them well.
A growing tree, ready to be planted in a forest by your worker.
A hammer and a mace, I’ll see what I can do.
A hard and durable metal. Used to make improved tools and weaopns. Smelted from Iron Ore in the Furnace
A hard and durable metal. Used to make improved tools and weaopns. Smelted from Iron Ore in the Furnace
A hard, crystalline mineral composed of silica.
A healthy dose of skepticism, I respect that. But you won't regret it. This isn't a fool's errand; it's an opportunity.
A heretic like you will never triumph over me!
A heretic like you will never triumph over me!
A letter from caring the father to his daughter.
A letter in an incomprehensible language, given to you by Paul Fishburn
A little birdy told me Oscar wanted to speak to you. He said it was something to do with the information you were looking for. You should go see him.
A little bit of everything, as long as it doesn’t involve fighting. If you think it’s a good idea, let me know! I’ll be right here.
A log harvested from softwood trees, such as pine and oak.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A merchant's life isn't easy, but at least I'm not a peasant toiling in the fields all day.
A more durable type of wood used in crafting and building. Requires better axe to harvest.
A much stronger gate type used in setting up defenses.
A much stronger wall type used in setting up defenses.
A mysterious figure, you say? That's bound to spark curiosity. I mean, mages and spirits don’t really exist, right?
A natural protein fiber, some forms of which can be woven into textiles.
A new purpose, huh? Just tell me when.
A new well? No small feat, indeed! For a job that size, let's say 20 gold per shovel oughta do it. They’re worth their weight in well, dirt, I suppose.
A nice old man asked me to deliver the letter to his daughter living in Farnworth, which seems like an easy coin. I’m sure nothing bad will happen.
A note that you found on one of the Brigands.
A nutritious staple crop with versatile culinary uses, providing sustenance and filling bellies. Can be grown on a farm.
A payment from a mysterious trader. I am sure this gold was acquired in a perfectly legal way.
A piece of an animal's coat, still covered in fur. Can be used in place of more complex textiles. Can be skinned from a wolf or rabbit.
A place for companions to prepare for journey by stocking up on gear and food from the settlement.
A place where you can get rid of items that aren't needed anymore.
A pleasure doing business with you.
A potion that helps heal wounds.
A potion that helps with pests.
A proof that there is one less thief in the Karvenia.
A puppet of the Queen, and a protegé of her late father.
A rare and valuable mineral found deep within the earth, nickle ore holds the promise of enhanced durability and resilience.
A refined material crafted from animal skins, meticulously prepared and treated, parchment becomes the canvas for illuminated manuscripts.

A revolutionary medium crafted from pulped plant fibers, paper transforms the medieval realm by providing a lighter, more accessible writing surface, allowing for the dissemination of knowledge, record-keeping, and the flowering of ideas through the written word.
A rivet is a permanent mechanical fastener. Before being installed, a rivet consists of a smooth cylindrical shaft with a head on one end.
A secret spice used by shamans from the North to invoke the spiritual world.
A shadowy figure… There must be a more reasonable explanation than some phantom haunting the village.
A simple bag of milled grain is very valuable in these hard times of food shortages.
A simple fence meant for setting up primitive defenses.
A single-stranded rope used in simple applications. Refined by a Weaver.
A single-stranded rope used in simple applications. Refined by a Weaver.
A small primitive source of heat for cooking basic foods. Requires wood to maintain the fire.
A smuggler has asked me to gather goods for her so she can resell them in Osterforde, and she’s planning to steal one of your boats. Just thought you should know.
A space to grow various plants with the help of a hoe, water and fertilizer.
A space to grow various plants.
A special glue stolen from the bandits.
A staple food in the Lowlands, available freely in most forests. Can be eaten raw, dried, or added to other ingredients to make better dishes.
A step up from a tent. Each house can hold three people.

A step up from the lowly Simple Workbench, the Advanced Workbench provides a much more suitable workspace. Here you can craft improved armors, tools, weapons and refined materials which will be critical for the success of your settlement.
A sticky and aromatic substance secreted by trees, resin serves as a valuable material for crafting. Can be processed from hard wood at Coal Burner.
A sticky and aromatic substance secreted by trees, resin serves as a valuable material for crafting. Can be processed from hard wood at Coal Burner.
A stranded Traveler on the road to Bradford asked me to be his escort due to the bandit's attacks and wild animals. I’ve decided to help him.
A stranded Traveler on the road to Bradford asked me to be his escort due to the bandit's attacks and wild animals. I’ve decided to help him.
A sturdy, wooden structure designed for early-warning and defense.
A tool resembling a spade with a broad blade. Used for moving earth and other materials.
A tool resembling a spade with a broad blade. Used for moving earth or other material.
A true lifesaver! Thank you, please take this as a reward. Now I need to excuse you, it's time to fix this damn door.
A versatile and abundant material from the earth. Can be used for building.
A versatile grain cultivated in paddies as a staple food, offering sustenance and nourishment.
A vibrant and crisp root vegetable with a peppery bite, radishes bring a refreshing crunch. Can be grown on a farm.
A week, maybe? He was quite sick recently, and the stress of it seemed to cause him to spiral. Some say he’ll be back to normal once he wins the election, but I’m not so certain.
A wise decision! Even a chance encounter with these relics could unveil secrets that only someone with your insight could decipher. I eagerly await your discoveries.
A woman from Padstow asked me to find her missing husband and his brother-in-law. She seemed worried, I hope nothing bad happened to them.Find the woman's missing husband.

A word of caution, though. The flames you've stoked may attract unwanted attention to your own lands. The brigands, scorned and furious, might divert their reclamation parties and launch raids upon your settlement.
A work area for separating wheat grain and straw.
A worker will collect the yields from placed animal traps and re-set them automatically.
A workshop where various gear and refined items can be crafted.
ALWAYS SHOW HEALTH BAR AND ICONS
Abandoned tool that you found next to the broken cart.
About his backbone or his blade? Messin’ aside, if I’m gonna fix this, I need you to bring me some copper ore.
About the grain you wanted me to deliver…
About the help with your caravan…
About the herbs you asked for...
About the materials you asked for...
About the money for the tools…
About the money for the tools…
About the pelts you asked for...
About the raw hide you asked for...
About the shovels you asked me for…
About the smoked meat you wanted…
About the straw you asked me for…
About the straw you asked me for…
About the stuff you asked for...
About the stuff you asked for…
About things you need to fix the mill…
About things you need to fix the mill…
About this quiver and arrows you wanted...
About this task you gave me…
About those herbs for the sick cow…
Absolute swindler, that one. I guess nothing comes cheap anymore.
Absolutely, I do love books. They've been my teachers and friends when I didn't have any.
Absolutely. There's much more to uncover about the beliefs and customs here. I'll keep exploring.
Actually I think I have no more questions.
Actually, I have some questions about Haerndean, if you don’t mind?
Actually, I wanted to ask you some questions about the election. If you don’t mind.
Actually, I was going to take a day off.
Actually, before I go… Marek mentioned you’re in pretty bad health. And you were struggling to pay your last apprentice?
Actually, most people have never met him. There’s even rumors he died a few years back. But I don’t believe it. Burning Stove would be long gone if it was just Klara in charge.
Actually, since you're here, there is something else.
Actually, while you’re here, I had an idea. After this whole bullying incident, I’ve decided I want to become brave like you, but I’m not sure where to start.
Actually, while you’re here, I had an idea. After this whole bullying incident, I’ve decided I want to become brave like you, but I’m not sure where to start.
After all these years… We are finally free. I feel so ashamed about our deal with Ashbourne. Thank you, protector, we have to do our best to regain our honor in the Lowlands.
After all these years… We are finally free. I feel so ashamed about our deal with Ashbourne. Thank you, protector, we have to do our best to regain our honor in the Lowlands.
After everything I’ve heard about you, and all you’ve done… I don’t want to believe you, because it means things are much worse than I thought. But I have no reason to doubt you.
After his passing, his wife gave me his book collection. There was a lot of information about the rebellion in there, the kind of books that are banned in the Lowlands.
After rescuing a member of Burning Stove, I’ve struck up an agreement with Klara Winterburn to free more of her people.
After rescuing a member of Burning Stove, I’ve struck up an agreement with Klara Winterburn to free more of her people.

After successfully negotiating with the hunter and learning to craft rabbit traps myself, I returned to Elder Dobron with the fur, hoping to have finally earned his trust. However, Dobron was not yet satisfied; he acknowledged my success but immediately presented me with a more dangerous task: dealing with a wolf problem threatening the village. Despite my reservations and the clear increase in danger, I agreed to hunt the wolves.
After the fight, he went to the tavern and got pissed. And on his way home, he tripped and fell. A branch punctured his arm when he tried to break the fall.
Ah Klara, she’s The Lady. She’s like a daughter I never had. I met her when she was still little. Her parents were killed by bandits, but she managed to escape them.
Ah here, don't be like that old fool, show some backbone.
Ah yes, I saw them near the forest at night, like some nocturnal creature.
Ah yes, the Lord of the Lowlands. He is one of the Queen’s peons, sent to oversee the people of the Lowlands.
Ah yes, the Lord of the Lowlands. He is one of the Queen’s peons, sent to oversee the people of the Lowlands.
Ah yes, yes, I saw you in at least one of my dreams, yes. How can this sage help you?
Ah, Blackridgepool. I once knew a young maiden from there, but that's a story for another time. Tell me what you've got.
Ah, Copper and Tin Ores, essential for any budding blacksmith. You see when you smelt one part tin with one part copper, you get bronze.
Ah, Flax and Hemp, the very backbone of our craft. Flax, with its fine fibers, is perfect for weaving into linen, making everything from clothes to bed sheets.
Ah, I see. This craftsmanship… It's meticulous and thoughtful. You've proven your worth today, well done.
Ah, I’ve heard a lot about you. It’s good to finally meet!
Ah, Sage, the guardian of the garden. It's not just for cooking; it's a healer's ally.
Ah, curious one, are you? These herbs possess unique properties that are potent against pests. One numbs their senses, another disrupts their appetite.
Ah, excellent work! Let's reflect on what we've uncovered.
Ah, how long have you got? Let's just say blacksmithing itself is much easier than dealing with the Ogden clan.
Ah, it's a task suited to someone of your discerning nature. Hidden across our wilds are ancient shrines, each one a repository of forgotten wisdom.

Ah, just the person I was hoping to see! You've got a knack for building, right? Our village is in a bind. We're seeing fewer travelers than we used to, and it's hurting business. I have an idea that could help, but I need someone with your skills.
Ah, my dear friend! Always a pleasure to see you.

Ah, planks are the backbone of carpentry. Strong and reliable for building anything from chicken coops to grand dining tables. Depending on how you cut and treat 'em, they can last generations or make a fine piece of décor.
Ah, so you've got ears after all. Yes, I was a champion, believe it or not. But now?
Ah, so you’re going to vote too! I’ll be honest with ye. I don’t have the flashy, well-traveled experience and stories of my opponent.
Ah, sorry, I keep forgetting you don’t know much about Burning Stove. The Butcher is our leader.
Ah, the notorious mystery savior of Karvenia. You’re very bold, stepping onto my grounds.
Ah, the old man and I have a history. Let's just say he's not my favorite villager. But I can't deny a request for help if I want to keep my good reputation, can I?
Ah, the smell of fresh herbs in the air always lifts my spirits. Welcome to my garden, adventurer!
Ah, the smell of fresh herbs in the air always lifts my spirits. Welcome to my garden, adventurer!
Ah, the smell of fresh herbs in the air always lifts my spirits. Welcome to my garden.
Ah, the smell of fresh herbs in the air always lifts my spirits. Welcome to my garden.
Ah, those wolves have been trouble for a while now. I'd like to help, but I'm out of the right herbs for a healing mix. If you can grab them, I can whip something up.
Ah, welcome, traveler. What brings you to our humble village?
Ah, yes. I see you’ve met our resident ghost. He wanders these lands in limbo, trying to contact his daughter… Only a few can see him.
Ah, you drive a hard bargain. But fine, I’ll increase your payment.
Ah, you know, farmers aren't too happy with how things are looking because of the brigands. They're controlling the amount of harvested wheat in the fields, which makes production really problematic.
Ah, you're a legend! It feels like I’ve been waiting an age to get my hands on this bait! That Golden Trout will be mine!
Ah, you're just the person I was hoping to run into today. My wife, bless her heart, has been poring over one of them new books Oscar brought in.
Ah, young one. Thank you again for your help in curing me, I’m feeling quite chipper again! How can I help you today?

Ahoy there, adventurer! Welcome to my humble abode in the wilds of the forest. I hope you're ready for a life of solitude, because let's be real, I'm the only one who's going to be talking to you out here. But hey, at least you won't have to worry about putting up with any pesky villagers.
Ain't easy with all the troubles these days. But you avengin' Bessie, that means a lot, it does. Got a bit o' coin here for your troubles.
Akin to a piece of charcoal. Made by keeping over a campfire for too long.
Alas, it was a job well done! Here’s your pay.
Aldekerk. Have you heard of the Theseus Mercenaries? I was one of them. At least, until I turned my back on them in a moment of weakness and arrogance.
Aleks Amanita said I could find a flask for mixing here?

Aleks Amanita saw the sigil on the cloak of someone who was arguing with Elder Dobbins. The Elder denied knowing anything. Someone is definitely lying, but I’m worried that by the time I find out who, it’ll be too late.
Aleks Amanita, a herbalist who lives in the forest, lent me one book about herbs and medicine, and I can’t wait to try them.
All I had to do was follow them when they left the tavern last night, and they led me to the gold at the end of the rainbow.
All I have is my word. It's saddening that, in these times, it may not be enough.
All because of a stupid fight… I shouldn't have let him go.
All right, but I’ll bring the sword, just in case.
All right, but don't wait too long, I've got a lot of work to do.
All right, give me the grain and I’ll get to it.
All right, it’s ready to give it to the old malcontent. And make sure to get paid! The old man’s memory can be very selective when it comes to owing money…
All villagers have fallen in battle
Allows easy transportation of seeds for planting.
Allows easy transportation of seeds for planting.
Allows fast traveling between two or more signs. You can't fast travel with your companions, when you are in combat, or if there's foes nearby.
Allows one worker to chop logs into wood.
Allows researching advanced buildings and items, and is a part of the Village Hall.
Allows settlers to gather fresh water whenever it's needed for the settlement.
Allows the wearer to carry 30 arrows of one type
Allows the wearer to carry 30 arrows of one type
Allows the wearer to carry a few items on a belt.
Allows the wearer to carry items on your back.
Allows to rest, craft basic items and cook some food during travels.
Allows to rest, craft basic items and cook some food during travels.
Allows you and your settlers to craft all the armors, tools, weapons and refined materials which will be critical for the success of your settlement.
Allows you and your settlers to craft all the basic gear along with refining logs.
Allows you and your settlers to craft all the basic gear along with refining logs.
Allows you and your settlers to craft all the basic gear along with refining logs.
Allows you to instantly heal some amount health. Better than Bandage.
Alright then, here you have it.
Alright, I will bring you this fertilizer.
Alright, I will pay you extra. But you need to keep quiet about it, deal?
Alright, I will see what I can do.
Alright, I'll bite. No pun intended. I’ll pay the elder a visit.
Alright, I'll help you. Let's go.
Alright, I'll keep looking. But I'm not holding my breath for any groundbreaking revelations.
Alright, I'll take a stab at it. Can't be worse than not getting any tools at all, right?
Alright, I'll try to hunt one down. Is there anything else I should know?
Alright, I’ll bring you some arrows.
Alright, I’ll see what I can do.
Alright, Paul. What's this big discovery you're talking about?
Alright, alright, I’ll gather what you need.
Alright, alright. Where did you see this ghost?
Alright, but what’s the point? We’re talking about ancient history here
Alright, calm yourself! I don’t want to cause a scene in the middle of the village!
Alright, fine. I'll help you for 50 gold coins.
Alright, give me the bag, but if I get stabbed I'm gonna haunt you from the beyond.
Alright, give me the list, and I will see what I can do.
Alright, give me the money, and I’ll keep my mouth shut.
Alright, it shouldn't take long. I'll take care of it.
Alright, tell me what you need.
Alright, the problem is I was attacked by a pack of wolves in the forest. In panic, I threw the sword at them and ran away.
Alright, what's on your shopping list? I'll go fetch 'em.
Also, I have these ones for you. Choose what you want.
Also, I hope you are not going by yourself.
Also, you will need to take the spices from a nearby herbalist. They’re essential.
Always a pleasure! Till next time.
Always appreciate the effort, mate. You've not only given the forge some fuel but also made life easier.
Always glad to help. What can I do for you?
Ambition got the best of me. During a competition for the title of the best blacksmith in the village, tried to sabotage another competitor.

Amelie has given me advice on what I should do next. Focusing solely on searching for answers is not the best idea. I need to make a bigger base of operations, hire some people, earn coins, and get some contacts in the Lowlands.

Amelie has given me advice on what I should do next. Focusing solely on searching for answers is not the best idea. I need to make a bigger base of operations, hire some people, earn coins, and get some contacts in the Lowlands.
Amelie said you had information for me. Let’s make it quick.

Amelie sold me on a plan to sow distrust among the Brigands by placing a stolen token from one of them in their secret stash. Even if this does not spark a new rebellion, we could have some fun watching them turn on each other.
Amelie told me that Padstow’s Elder is in need of help. I should speak with him.
Amelie was right about you, you really love to talk.
Amusing. If I give the signal, an arrow would shoot through your eye before you even realize it.
An ancient ruin? That's quite the find.
An axe crudely made from a shaped chunk of iron. Used as a tool for woodcutting, but can also be used for combat.
Ancient shrines, you say? Sounds like something worth learning about. Where should I start?
And Farnworth's honors Devannos, a warrior deity.
And Gods forbid we miss our quota. The punishment would be worse than the terrible working conditions!
And I assume you don’t know who these Brigands were, or where I can find them?
And I don’t want to travel to another village at the moment. Things are tense out there, I wouldn’t feel safe.
And I expect the grain. Give it up now, or we’ll stop being so nice.
And I happen to know where they keep their secret stash, where they hide loot from their leaders. Place the token in there, and let the accusations fly.
And I need you to shut up and give me the bag.
And I want you to know, I try to take care of those who cooperate with me. So if I can help you in any way, just ask.
And I’ll admit, she is a little naive, bless her. But her heart’s in the right place, and that’s what people care about right now.
And I’ll kill whoever I want! <Fight>
And I’m guessing you couldn’t agree on that little issue.
And I’m not interested in working with a humorless donkey, but I don’t have any other options, unfortunately.
And Thiana… Thiana got killed in the chaos. By Loxley’s sword.

And as we face more challenges in the future, I believe compromise and cooperation is vital. As much as we may want to vilify Lord Ashbourne, he is the best chance we have of overcoming our obstacles.
And be careful, the boars are vicious.
And before you ask yes he is, or was, a boar. He was my pet, I kept it a secret. If the village had known I was protecting a full-grown boar, a source of food, it would’ve been disastrous.
And believe me, the emigration process is worse than torture.
And by the Gods, be careful when showing off that sigil. You might run into someone willing to kill to protect its secrets…
And defying that rule is more than my job here is worth. Speak to him first.
And don’t forget their tusk I beg you!
And don’t forget their tusk I beg you!
And even the air tastes sweeter… but still we need to be careful. The brigands will not forget. They will yearn to reclaim what was stripped from them.
And everything went smoothly, the trader gave me the bag and he said he would contact you later.
And feathers, they're not just for arrows; they can add flair to hats or be used in bedding for a soft night's sleep.
And feathers? Best for fletching arrows or stuffing for your bedding.
And feel free to take one more.
And for someone who wasn't born here, like myself, it's a way to show my unwavering commitment to our community's values!
And here's a little bonus for you.
And here, take these coins, I'm grateful for the help.
And how’s your husband dealing with this little… setback?
And if you don't play by their rules, they make life hard for you. That's why I keep to myself, stay out of their waters.
And if you’re feeling under the weather, a bit of garlic in hot broth will set you right quicker than you can say 'witchcraft'.
And is that all you remember? You didn’t see the person’s face?
And it was Ashbourne who chose who’d be inspected. He was trying to take Atwood supporters out of the equation.
And lie to her for the rest of our lives? What if someone discovers the truth?
And maybe it’ll show the anglers of the Lowlands that we don’t need that damn Fishing Guild…
And mud? It's not just for slinging. Packed tight, it can shore up a wall or fill gaps in a thatch roof, keeping you dry and cozy.
And now that the Elder has accepted you into the village, at least tentatively, we can move without drawing too much suspicion. We just need to be careful.
And on top of that, Ashbourne’s vote is somehow worth three times more than anyone else’s? It all smells rotten.
And one more thing, bring two pieces of fried meat. My wife loves it and I forgot about it and now I want to give her a little surprise.
And one more thing: the bandits like to hoard many materials they don’t use, so search their camp thoroughly.
And please take this as a token of our gratitude.
And some bonus from the villagers.
And something extra for a job well done.
And something extra from the villagers.
And take one of these as a token of my gratitude.
And that’s my situation. Do you want to hear about my proposition now?
And the thing is, the Brigands were being very polite. Cordial, even. Behavior I’ve never received from Brigands. They slapped a big bag of gold on the table, and I gave them the cloak.
And then I can capture him. The people can decide his fate.
And then I’ll kill him, like he deserves.

Attention, brave citizens!<cf><cf>A shadow stalks our land, known only as THE BUTCHER. This nefarious fiend strikes fear into the hearts of all, for he is not only dangerous but delights in the abduction of innocent villagers.<cf><cf>His presence is a blight upon our realm, his actions a stain upon our honor. Blackridgepool is a peaceful village known for its fertile soil and lush surroundings. The first settlers came from other parts of the Lowlands, attracted by the good soil and the unique herbs and plants found near the swamps.<cf><cf>Over the years, the village has become a major crop producer and exporter in the region, thanks to advanced farming practices. This caused the village to thrive as more people were drawn to the village's fields and close-knit community. Visitors are always welcomed, especially those interested in trade. The villagers take pride in their flavorful, high-quality food, sourced from the surrounding fields and animal enclosures.<cf><cf>To meet the growing demand caused by the scourge, farmers have started draining swamps to create more usable land. They’re starting to see lower yields during their harvests, but they’re yet to be as affected as other villages in the Lowlands.
Blackridgepool’s harvest celebration is a serious celebration, so I’ll try my best to help them. The people of the Highlands need to have uplifting events during these troublesome times.

Bless us, O Feared Gods. May your altar overflow with bounty, just like our lands. Protect our people, bring hope for better mornings, till the blood pours from our veins or death will take us back home.

Bradford is a young village located between the woods and a stream. It was founded by travelers from other regions in Karvenia. The brother of Padstow's Elder, Franciszek, and his partner Andrea chose the location since it seemed ideal for hunting and gathering herbs.<cf><cf>After working with a neighboring village to craft bows and arrows, the Elders of each village decided to merge their people, and Bradford was established. The new village flourished through the sale of meat and other byproducts from their hunts.
Bradford might be a lost cause, but Blackridgepool is having its own election very soon.

Bradford was originally founded by fur trappers from another country, who came to the Lowlands in search of prey to hunt for their business. However, it came as a shock to them when they found Farnworth already had the monopoly on quality furs in the Lowlands.<cf><cf>So, in an effort to gain revenge and devastate the Farnworth economy, the settlers of Bradford began teaching themselves how to be elite archers. They weren’t planning to attack Farnworth; they wanted to hunt in Farnworth territory, leaving the fur sellers in Farnworth with no prey to gather their wares from.<cf><cf>Initially, the plan was a great success, as the Bradford archers were able to hunt down all the bears and foxes before the Farnworth fur trappers could. However, their plan had one minor flaw: although they had become efficient and lethal with arrows, they had never learned how to make more arrows. Once they ran out of arrows after a fortnight, they were forced to apologize to the people of Farnworth and sign a peace treaty.<cf><cf>The people of Bradford have retained their reputation as skilled archers, but the relationship with Farnworth is still tense to this very day.

Bradford? As far as I know, at least from legends of the Lowlands, it was founded by travelers from other regions in Karvenia. Franciszek, the brother of Padstow's Elder, and his partner Andrea chose this location for its hunting and herb-gathering potential.
Bradford’s situation with copper is still dire due to the election, could you find a moment to help us?

Brave Hunters of Bradford!<cf><cf>In the heart of the wild, amidst the echoing calls of the forest, stands our valiant leader, Lord Ashbourne, a stalwart defender of our territory and way of life. Let your spirits rise with pride as we honor his unwavering courage and tireless dedication to our beloved Bradford.<cf><cf>Lord Ashbourne, a beacon of strength and resilience, has led countless battles to safeguard our borders and preserve our hunting grounds. His recent triumph in repelling a fierce incursion from our adversaries speaks volumes of his strategic prowess and unwavering resolve.<cf><cf>Deeply ingrained in the annals of Bradford's history, Lord Ashbourne is the epitome of bravery and leadership. From leading daring hunts to ensuring the prosperity of our community, he remains our unwavering protector and guide.<cf><cf>Let us raise our voices in praise and gratitude for Lord Ashbourne, whose bravery knows no bounds and whose commitment inspires us all. May his legacy endure for generations to come, a testament to the indomitable spirit of our people.<cf><cf>Long live Lord Ashbourne! May his name be forever etched in the annals of our proud hunters' heritage!<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

Brew it in a tea to soothe sore throats or mix it into salves for cuts and bruises.

Brigands. They set a trap after ambushing the farmer. All over a bag of grain.
Brilliant! Best get to work on the treatment right away, so.
Brilliant. It’s going to be anarchy, especially if they’re drinking tonight. They might even start killing each other!

But all is not lost. Take this to blacksmith Finton Mallet in Horndean, he’s the best weaponsmith I know. Maybe he will figure something out.
But anyone trying to leave gets stopped and thrown into a cell. Ashbourne makes us work everyday to keep us weak. Many die from dehydration or illness.
But anyway, I’d appreciate it if you got to work on that.
But anyway, bring me these herbs and I will brew something that should help.

But if we start producing our own, then it will be better for the Lowlands and its economy!
But if we start producing our own, then it will be better for the Lowlands and its economy!

Citizens of the realm!<cf><cf>Beware! A shadow lurks amongst us, a figure known only as THE BUTCHER. This vile traitor of the crown and Lord Ashbourne has stained our land with the blood of the innocent. With his own hands, he has snuffed out lives in fits of rage and fury.<cf><cf>But fear not, for we shall not rest until this menace is brought to justice! We call upon the brave souls of our kingdom to keep watch and be vigilant. If you should lay eyes upon this wretched fiend, do not approach him. Instead, swiftly inform the brigands of his whereabouts.<cf><cf>Together, we shall root out this treachery and restore peace to our land. Let the name of THE BUTCHER be known to all, and may he be brought to justice swiftly!<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

Dear Fintan,<cf><cf>How are you, my old friend? Well, I hope. Unfortunately, I cannot say the same. I’m writing to you to ask for a favor. If you have any weapons, tools, or materials to spare, I beg that you send them to my workshop in the Gauntlet.<cf><cf>The monarchy is growing more paranoid by the day. They believe another rebellion is on the horizon, and have decided to confiscate all the weapons and armor I have made this year. Furthermore, the royal guard have become more aggressive and callous, knowing we are unarmed and cannot fight back.<cf><cf>Please, anything you can spare will be a godsend. We need to be able to protect ourselves from these tyrants.<cf><cf>And make sure you take care of yourself, and your village. The Queen’s paranoia and cruelty may yet push people into rebelling. It is becoming a self-fulfilling prophecy.<cf><cf>Your friend,<cf>J.G.

Dear scum,<cf><cf>I know what you’re doing! You’re selling your products at a loss to put me out of business, because you can afford it! I knew you were cruel, but I never thought you would sink this low!<cf><cf>If you don’t adjust your prices to the correct market rate, I will call in a favor from my friends in the Royal Bastards, and they might just pay you a visit in the middle of the night!<cf><cf>This will be your only warning, I suggest you take it seriously!<cf><cf>I.F.

Denizens of Bradford,<cf><cf>In accordance with the decrees of the Council of Great Lord Ashbourne, we hereby proclaim an adjustment in tax rates, effective immediately, to fortify the prosperity of our esteemed village. The revenues collected shall be directed towards vital services and infrastructure development, tailored to the needs of our skilled hunters and trappers.<cf><cf>Hunters shall contribute a portion of their game, while trappers shall provide a percentage of their furs. These funds will be allocated towards the maintenance of hunting grounds, the procurement of essential supplies, and the fortification of our village defenses against external threats.<cf><cf>Your compliance with these tax obligations, reflective of your esteemed profession, is essential for the continued growth and security of Bradford.<cf><cf>Rest assured, every contribution will be utilized judiciously to safeguard our way of life and ensure a thriving future for all who call Bradford home.<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

Denizens of The Lowlands!<cf><cf>The grand Solstice doth draw nigh, and with it, the esteemed Lord Ashbourne extends a heartfelt invitation to a celebration of momentous proportions. Distinguished guests from the farthest reaches of Karvenia shall grace our beloved lands, gathering under the watchful eyes of our noble Lord.<cf><cf>In anticipation of this auspicious occasion, a decree resonates from the very heart of Ashbourne: let cleanliness be our guiding virtue. May thine homes gleam with pristine splendor, villages sparkle like stars in the night, and thyself be cleansed with the utmost diligence. Pray, keep thy animals at a distance, ensuring the festivities unfold without disruption.<cf><cf>Be forewarned, for those who neglect these solemn instructions shall face dire consequences. In his wisdom, Lord Ashbourne hath decreed that only those who uphold the sanctity of cleanliness shall partake in the joyous revelry. Those who dare to transgress shall bear the weight of punishment, serving as a solemn reminder that in Ashbourne, order and decorum reign supreme.<cf><cf>As the grand Solstice casts its radiant glow upon our lands, let Ashbourne stand as a beacon of grace and distinction in the eyes of all who gather from across the realm. May the festivities be blessed, and the spirit of unity prevail in our cherished home.<cf><cf>Signed,<cf>Lieutenant Berthold Kug.

Denizens of The Lowlands!<cf><cf>With great pride and admiration, we declare that the once-menacing specter of banditry has been vanquished from our lands, thanks to the resolute efforts of our noble Lord Ashbourne. By the royal decree of Queen Ossene, Lord Ashbourne took up the mantle of protector, leading our brave forces against the marauding bandits that once plagued our kingdom. Through strategic brilliance and unyielding courage, these outlaws have been scattered to the winds, and their threat extinguished like a flickering flame.<cf><cf>Signed,<cf>The Council of the Great Lord Ashbourne.

Denizens of The Lowlands!<cf><cf>Your Lord, Lord Ashbourne, has decreed rewards for those who expose collaborators against our realm. Farnworth and Bradford have a longstanding feud over hunting grounds, dating back a century.<cf>In fact, Farnworth’s founders used to be part of Bradford. The conflict began when a group of hunters found Bradford's methods primitive and decided to settle on the other side of the Lowlands. Farnworth exports leather and fur, while Bradford focuses on bones and meat, creating intense competition for both sides that has literally become a fight for survival.<cf>Farnworth thrived with the use of more advanced tools, attracting traders to the village’s tavern. The rivalry persisted for decades until a truce was called to fight a boar invasion, but their mutual dislike remains unchanged to this day.

Fuzzy memories of her fill my head as I stand here, lost in thought. They're like fragments of a distant dream, fading with time. I strain to recall more, but all that surfaces are glimpses – the blue of her eyes, a faint hint of her familiar scent, and the soft song she'd sing when she'd tuck me into bed.<cf><cf>Regret weighs on me. There was no proper goodbye. When I had to flee Karvenia, I needed to see her one last time. I called out for her, but she wasn't there.<cf>Now, all that's left is this emptiness. Haerndean and Horndean are two villages with contrasting histories and traditions. Horndean was founded by the oldest clan in the Lowlands, seeking a peaceful life near a stream where they could fish and mine for iron.<cf><cf>While villagers excelled in blacksmithing, they eventually needed to rely on Haling Cove for more iron. This caused tensions to grow between Horndean and Haerndean, since Haerndean was steadfast in its rejection of monarchy and Haling Cove was in support of it.<cf><cf>Horndean became a village loyal to the monarchy, while Haerndean remains proud of their free heritage and traditions.

Haerndean is believed to be the oldest settled village in the Lowlands, but conflict was sown into its very foundation. Within years of its establishment, the settlers of Haerndean became divided on the growth of their village. Some wanted to expand north, while others wanted to stay closer to the valley.<cf><cf>“My grandmother told me stories about the early settlers,” recalls Rosetta Omari, a 101-year-old woman who’s spent her entire life in Haerndean. “The folks here barely had enough buildings to shelter everyone, and yet they still bickered day and night about the future of the village. They decided to appoint a first elder, for leadership. But those who wanted to push north sabotaged the election, and a chicken ended up as an elder. Not a figure of speech, a literal chicken.”<cf><cf>The chicken was said to be an uninspiring, indecisive leader, and a human took over the Elder post a month later.
Haerndean is free, but our work is far from over. It's not yet liberated.

Haerndean is the oldest village in the Lowlands, settled by proud and independent people seeking fertile land and freedom.<cf>Despite limited prosperity, Haerndean remained peaceful, living off the land with basic tools.<cf><cf>When the first Lord of the Lowlands, a man named Robert Copue, arrived in the region, Haerndean strongly opposed collaborating with authority. This ultimately led to a rift with the neighboring village of Horndean, and animosity between the two villages still persists to this day.
Haerndean's shrine speaks of humble offerings.
Haerndean, and all of the Lowlands, is going through a difficult time. We’re hungry, getting taxed into poverty, and dealing with threats from bandits and brigands.
Haerndean, and all of the Lowlands, is going through a difficult time. We’re hungry, getting taxed into poverty, and dealing with threats from bandits and brigands.
Hah! Old Dalibor found another apprentice to run into the ground, huh? I suppose I can help you, but remember, nothing comes for free in the Lowlands.

Haha, if he’s asking you to gather logs, sounds like his back is playing up again. A far cry from his heyday!

Haling Cove was once a work camp occupied by prisoners and supervising soldiers living in basic huts. The prisoners worked the mines day in and day out for iron. Conditions were harsh, and many fell ill and died. The first Lord of Lowlands saw the cruel way the prisoners were treated, and made the necessary changes for Haling Cove to become a small village where people could live relatively well.<cf><cf>Over time, prisoners were joined by free workers that came to Haling Cove to earn money. Soldiers still watched over the village, and a fence and towers were built. Villagers needed permission to leave at night, creating some resentment within the community.<cf><cf>Despite the restrictions, Haling Cove thrived. It became a welcoming community, open to visitors who follow safety regulations.

Hemp is the workhorse, good for the tougher jobs—twill for bags, ropes for tying, even gear for the harder roads. Each plant, a treasure in its own right, with care and skill becomes a part of daily life.
Hemp prefers the shade of the forest's edge but can be grown on your farm with a bit of care. Both require patience and a green thumb to yield their valuable fibers.
Hemp, now, that's strength—used for making durable textiles, ropes, and even canvas for sails. Both have their place in weaving tales as much as textiles.
Hemp, on the other hand, is tougher, and ideal for ropes, sacks, and even some hardy clothing. Both are indispensable in a weaver's hands.

I am the caretaker of this belltower, entrusted with its maintenance and protection. My duty has been to ensure that the bell atop this tower serves its purpose: to warn the people of Northern Descent and neighboring villages of impending danger.<cf><cf>For years, I have stood watch over this land from the heights of this tower. It has been my responsibility to keep the bell in working order, ready to ring out its warning at the first sign of trouble. But now, as I pen these words, the tower lies in ruins around me, a testament to the passage of time and the neglect of our once-vigilant community.<cf><cf>I write this note in the hopes that someone will stumble upon it and learn from our mistakes. Our failure to maintain this belltower has left us vulnerable to the dangers that now threaten to engulf us. I can only pray that whoever finds this note will take up the mantle of responsibility and restore this tower to its former glory.<cf><cf>May this belltower once again serve as a beacon of warning, guiding the people of The Lowlands through the storms that lie ahead.

I got the pelts from the wolves, as requested. They won’t be a problem anymore.

I guess I have a little time for that. I was born and raised right here in Padstow. For two decades now, I've shouldered the responsibility of guiding our village through the challenges of hunger and bandit raids from the Lowlands.
I guess I’ve already come this far. I’ll see if I can find Badcocke’s stash.
I guess you weren't that bad after all. I will give you one more chance.

I hate them too, believe me. Back to my other questions.

I have a duty to my village first and foremost. I need to know who you are and why you came here.
I have a job for a traveler with a strong back, just like you.

I have a lot of years of practice, I know what I'm doing.
I have a proposition for you, if you want to earn a little coin.

I have no time for strangers, I need to work.
I have no time for this.
I have no time for this.

I have purged the first heretic, one of the Lord’s guards. I think he was growing suspicious of my work, but it has been dealt with. My first kill, and my heart is still racing. I can’t wait to visit my ancestor’s grave, and tell him what I’ve done. I hope this will inspire the people of Padstow to stand up for themselves in the future.

I inherited the mill from my grandfather and continued his legacy. My skills in milling grains have been invaluable to the village, providing fresh flour for its residents. Despite my work, I always make time to support the local community.

The idea was that these signs would guide more travelers to our village, potentially revitalizing local businesses that have been missing out on new customers. The promise of a reward and the chance to contribute positively to the community's welfare nudged me into action.

Padstow is a village in the Lowlands that was founded by a group of travelers.<cf>They settled in an area close to forests and fields, allowing them to continue their work as carpenters and farmers. A lumberjack called Padowski became the first Elder of the village, and under his guidance the villagers built beautiful homes and created a peaceful community.<cf><cf>But one night, Padowski was found dead with a knife in his back. His son Henry took his place as Elder. The village went on to persevere for centuries, hailed as a community built on hard work and determination.
Padstow merchant asked me to bring him booze from his friend Herbalist. He gave you a secret spice - necessary ingredient for the Booze.

Padstow was founded by a group of travelers who sought a place close to nature. They settled here, near the forests and fields. A remarkable lumberjack named Padowski emerged as the first Elder of Padstow.

People of Crasmere,<cf><cf>By the decree of the Council, under the discerning eye of Great Lord Ashbourne, we announce the forthcoming election to appoint the Elder in the Village of Bradford. Among the candidates for this esteemed position stands our current Elder, Patryk Sten, celebrated for his unwavering valor and prudent leadership.<cf><cf>We call upon you, esteemed residents of Crasmere, to heed this summons with solemnity. Let this proclamation resonate across every dock and cottage, ensuring that each of you grasps the profound significance of this decision that shall chart the course of our beloved land.<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

People of Padstow!<cf><cf>Great Lord Ashbourne, unwavering in bravery and unmatched in merit, stands as a beacon of strength and loyalty to our land. Let pride fill your hearts as you recall his countless deeds and sacrifices for our community.<cf><cf>Recently, Lord Ashbourne led a daring expedition to repel a bandit raid on our village, bravely confronting the threat and ensuring the safety of our people. His heroic actions in the face of danger are a testament to his unwavering commitment to our protection.<cf><cf>Lord Ashbourne, deeply rooted in our village's history, defended our land with unwavering courage. From securing borders to guiding us through trials, he remains a steadfast guardian of our dreams.<cf><cf>His bravery knows no bounds, his dedication a model for us all. Let pride swell as we behold his banner, bringing tidings of peace and prosperity.<cf><cf>Long live Lord Ashbourne! May his legacy endure in our hearts! May pride always adorn the Padstow board as a symbol of our gratitude and respect for our esteemed leader.<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

People of the Lowlands,<cf><cf>It is with great import that we announce the upcoming election for the position of our esteemed Elder in Village Bradford. One of the candidates for this vital role is none other than our current Elder, Patryk Sten, who is known for his courage and wisdom, vying for your vote against his electoral opposition.<cf><cf>Candidates, express your noble intentions at the village square, the heart of our community. Your voices will be heard when the time is right.<cf><cf>This pivotal decision will carve the destiny of our beloved Bradford. Spread this message across The Lowlands, let every villager know about it.<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourn, ruler of the Lowlands.

Resilient Farmers of Blackridgepool!<cf><cf>Under the steadfast guidance of Lord Ashbourne, our protector and leader, we thrive amidst the fields of Blackridgepool. His unwavering courage and tireless commitment ensure our prosperity.<cf><cf>Lord Ashbourne's recent triumph in repelling threats to our land stands as a testament to his strength and leadership. Embedded in our history, he epitomizes bravery and resilience, guiding us through challenges with unwavering resolve.<cf><cf>Let us honor Lord Ashbourne's valor and dedication, ensuring his name echoes through Blackridgepool's proud farming legacy.<cf>Long live Lord Ashbourne! Resilient people of Crasmere!<cf><cf>Along the shores of Crasmere, where the gentle lapping of waves meets the bustling rhythm of the fishing boats, we stand united under the auspicious leadership of Lord Ashbourne, our steadfast protector and advocate. Let us extol his tireless efforts, which have brought prosperity and abundance to our humble village.<cf><cf>Lord Ashbourne, a beacon of strength and foresight, has spearheaded initiatives to bolster our fishing industry and enhance our village's vitality. Through his leadership, Crasmere has flourished, with plentiful catches sustaining our community and fostering a sense of camaraderie among our fishermen.<cf><cf>Embedded in the fabric of Crasmere's identity, Lord Ashbourne symbolizes unity and prosperity. His unwavering dedication has transformed our village into a beacon of hope and resilience along the coastal shores.<cf><cf>Let us raise our voices in gratitude and admiration for Lord Ashbourne, whose vision and leadership have brought unprecedented blessings to Crasmere. May his legacy of progress and prosperity endure for generations to come, a testament to the strength and unity of our fishing village.<cf><cf>Long live Lord Ashbourne! May his guiding light continue to enrich the lives of all who call Crasmere home!<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

Respected Smiths and Miners of Horndean,<cf><cf>Under the auspices of the Council of Great Lord Ashbourne, we issue this decree regarding an adjustment in tax rates, effective immediately, to bolster the prosperity and security of our esteemed village.<cf><cf>The revenues collected will be directed towards vital services, infrastructure development, and initiatives aimed at augmenting the welfare of Hornden's skilled craftsmen and miners.<cf><cf>Forgers shall contribute a portion of their crafted goods, while miners shall provide a percentage of their yields, reflective of their esteemed professions.<cf><cf>These funds will be allocated towards the maintenance of mines, the acquisition of essential resources, and the fortification of our village's defenses against external threats.<cf><cf>Your compliance with these tax obligations is paramount for the continued growth and security of Horndean. Rest assured, every contribution will be utilized judiciously to safeguard our way of life and ensure a prosperous future for all who call Horndean home.<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne

Seeds serve as the foundational element for cultivating a variety of plants within the game. Players can acquire seeds either through the act of harvesting, where they are collected alongside the plants, or by purchasing them directly from Herbalists. These seeds are essential for players looking to engage in agriculture, allowing for the growth of diverse flora that can be used for various purposes within the game world.

Ten years ago, tragedy struck the kingdom of Karvenia when Prince Vuldar was killed by a stray arrow while hunting with his sister, Princess Osenne, who pointed the blame at a young child that was in the area at the time.<cf><cf>Fueled by his fury and grief, King Roan ordered the execution of every child who matched the description of the one who killed his only son. In retaliation, a counter-movement was formed to protect the children of Karvenia.<cf><cf>To ensure a safe future for the youth of their county, the rebels planned to march on the Gauntlet, Karvenia’s capital, to take the King's head. However, tempted by promises of gold by the King, an unknown member of the rebellion helped set an ambush that seemingly wiped out the rebellion leaders.<cf><cf>After the betrayal, the rebellion fell apart, and a brutal time followed as the King died and was succeeded by Queen Osenne in the years following.<cf><cf>The final death toll of the rebellion is believed to be in the hundreds.

The Lowlands are the poorest of the five regions, and one of the oldest, its occupants relying mainly on agriculture for their livelihood. Due to the scourge, the people are barely producing enough to sell, let alone to keep for themselves. The current controlling Lord Ashbourne, is cruel, and likes to raise the already high taxes above what the Queen orders to give himself a generous cut.<cf><cf>It was once a beautiful, flourishing place. The lifeblood of medieval sustenance, these bountiful crops serve as the foundation for bread, porridge, and other hearty meals. Can be grown on on a farm.

There's a rumor circulating about Lord Ashbourn's new hobby. They say he's started collecting strange stuffed animals, like trophies, and filling his castle with them, but that's nothing unusual. Folks are also saying that he likes to keep stuffed enemies as trophies.
There's been some talk about strange sightings along the coast. Folks have been saying they've seen shadowy figures lurking near the shoreline, especially during the night.

There's been talk of a secret prison hidden away in the Highlands, rumored to be under the control of Lord Ashbourne. They say it's a place of dread, where the unfortunate souls who cross the Lord's path are whisked away, never to be seen again.

There's been whispers about Dimwater Marsh and Drenchwood Grove. Some claim they're infested with bandits, their faces hidden behind masks, waiting to prey on unsuspecting wanderers. It sends shivers down my spine just thinking about it.

There's been whispers of Lord Ashbourn hosting a game for his loyal people. They say it involves a deadly hunt in the castle grounds, with the Lord himself leading the pursuit. The prize? Survival, if you're lucky. It's like something out of a twisted fairy tale.

There's nothing too much to say about me. I was born here and I might also die in this village. I'm a proud owner of this tavern. You can sleep here for some amount of coins, it's not much but it will keep you safe from wild life.
There's something I'd like to ask you.

There's talk of a rebellion gaining strength. They say more and more villagers are joining the cause, tired of living under the thumb of the nobles. It's a risky move, but it seems like they're not backing down anytime soon.
There, amidst the ancient rocks, is where you’ll mine the finest granite, but don’t forget your pickaxe!
There’s a bandit who’s been harassing me for an awfully long time.
There’s a hermit nearby who has a remedy. But I can't make the journey myself anymore.
There’s also a thing that, gods forbid, if I die on the road or just can’t make it back, the village won’t have no carpenter. Up the creek without a paddle, know what I mean?
There’s been whispers lately, some weird things are happening at night near Haerndean.
There’s even a bounty on his head, though it’s not much, since his crimes aren’t particularly egregious.
There’s no need to throw out threats. I'm looking for someone too.
There’s no time for pleasantries. I’m here with a warning.

There’s not much to tell! I was born here, and I’ve never left, apart from a few journeys to meet with other village Elders. I was appointed the Elder twenty years ago, and I take great pride in fulfilling my role here!
There’s some people. But… I don’t know if I can trust you.
There’s something going on in Blackridgepool. Something bad.
There’s something suspicious about the outcome of the Bradford Elder election. Amelia and I are going to investigate.
These are dangerous times, and this is a dangerous place. Bandits lie in wait for any vulnerability to exploit.
These are dangerous times, and weapons are getting more dangerous. We’ll need to rethink how we treat wounds and illness in the near future.
These are indeed impressive. And this letter, a proposal for a trading alliance, I see. A thoughtful gesture from Elder Yaxley.
These are tough times we live in, but a bit of coin can still buy you some comfort.
These are tough times we live in, but a bit of coin can still buy you some comfort.

These drunkards were attacked by wolves. Obviously.
These experiments don't sound very ethical, but I hope it turns out for the good of the Lowlands.
These lands are my home. How do I know you won't bring trouble to them?
These look sturdy enough. You've done a fine job, here’s your pay.
These shovels will help us a great deal. Thank you for your swift assistance.
These tools made of iron are essential for crafting and researching. Can be found as loot and produced by a Toolmaker.
These tools made of iron are essential for crafting and researching. Can be found as loot and produced by a Toolmaker.
These woods have their own character. It's a mix of serenity and danger. Bandits don't make it any easier.
They all think they can do what I do. But they don’t know what it’s like to be in this position, and to make the decisions I have to make. It’s not an easy life.
They beat me and stole my tools. Thank the Gods, they were too stupid to take the most valuable ones. .
They can come. We will be ready to write the next chapters, and they will speak of our resilience.
They come to me when I dream. When I perform worship, I see them, they speak and I listen and they tell me - to preserve old ways, pass knowledge.
They didn’t respond positively to my sense of humor.
They finished for the day, had dinner and a bath, and went to bed. In the morning, the farmer’s throat was cut.
They found me, didn’t they? Just showed up to my workshop one day.
They grow near the old well, outside the village. It's a bit of a trek, and well. I don’t have the stamina I used to.
They grow near the old well, outside the village. It's a bit of a trek, and well. I don’t have the stamina I used to.
They kidnapped me when I was trying to cross the border. I just needed to get out of here. This country is heading towards ruin, and nobody can see it.
They might not be as exciting as swords, but a good shovel can be a man's best friend. You know, next to a hound…
They nicked everything, including my glue. So I might need you to get it back.
They say he could catch fish where others couldn’t even get a nibble. He knew every nook and cranny of these rivers and lakes.
They say the first rule of mining is to never start a cave-in. The second rule is to never talk about the first rule.
They say this mystery person hasn’t actually done anything to the villagers, so it's not their problem.
They should be easy to find. The Brigands’ laziness made them so cocky that the Bastards made their camp next to the village.
They should have arrived by now, but none of the crew have contacted. I'm starting to get worried.
They should, yes. But lately, they are ignoring our needs and only collect taxes, at least the Brigands are not harassing us.
They stock the rivers and lakes, but only where it benefits them. And don't get me started on their 'protection' fees.
They strut around like they own every river and stream, bullying folks and hoarding the best fishing spots.
They used to be. But more and more people are turning on Ashbourne, which means they’re challenging the current Elders.
They wanted to buy a load of weapons for a good price.
They were many… we were many. They were worshiped, bellowed, feared, now all but forgotten, only I stayed.
They were no match for me.
They were paying well for my work, so I didn’t ask many questions.
They were unlucky, this forest is full of wolves...
They were wearing a cloak. A cloak bearing the very sigil you’ve been searching for.
They will come, first to collect their blood taxes, then for vengeance when they see their garrison defeated.
They will find us ready. Haerndean shall be liberated, no matter the cost.
They will not take the defeat of their garrison without retaliation.
They won’t find me hiding among villagers close to the Brigands.
They'll keep you warm through the harshest winter. Feathers, though, they're for the finer things—arrow fletching or decorations.
They're scattered across the valley, mostly hidden under large rocks or within the copper veins of our mountains.
They're the bane of any true fisherman's existence. They muscle in, claiming to uphold traditions, but really, they're squeezing us dry.
They've been a thorn in everyone's side. But that's not all. Folks can't help but grumble about the Queen, too. Seems her decisions don't sit well with many.
They've been a thorn in everyone's side. But that's not all. Folks can't help but grumble about the Queen, too. Seems her decisions don't sit well with many.
They’ll pay for what they’ve done! I swear it!
They’re a strange group. Very dangerous. But they don’t care about politics. Kidnapping for a ransom is in their wheelhouse.
They’re just bandits, I can take care of myself.
They’ve resorted to killing Elder candidates simply for saying no…
Thiana Reesling is running against the Elder. She’s something else, let me tell you. I don’t agree with her on everything, but she’s razor sharp, and steadfast in her principles. I respect that.
Thiana confronted Dobbins over the possible election corruption, and things got out of hand.
Thiana? She’s a good egg. Earnest to a fault, to be honest.
Think about it. Whatever it is, it’s scaring people at night, and two of us have seen it…
Thinking about it, huh? In my day, we acted, didn’t just think. Fine, do what you must.
This better be good, Oscar. What’s your angle this time?
This better be worth the hassle.
This can be used to farm Grain as long as the soil is suitable. Requires watering and the growing process can be sped up by using fertilizer.
This character has a broken arm - his fighting and production capabilities are significantly reduced
This character has a broken leg - his speed and mobility are significantly reduced!
This character has a head injury - confusion and pain significantly reduce his fighting and production capabilities!
This character has a punctured lung - he struggles to catch his breath and is mobility is significantly reduced.
This character has an arrow in the eye - his fighting and production capabilities are significantly reduced!
This character has broken ribs - his mobility is significant impacted and he struggles to take deep breaths!
This character has but a flesh wound.
This has been a complete waste of time…
This information could be crucial. Keep searching, and let's see what else we can unearth.
This inn's been my life for years. I cook, I clean, I listen to tales from far-off lands. You could say I'm a jack of all trades, with stories to tell and ears to listen.
This is a lot to take in. I need some time.
This is actually happening. We're making history
This is not a career where you learn on the job, because that would be disastrous. If he wants to be my protege, I’ll allow it. But he is not ready for this role.
This is not funny, you know.
This is wonderful, thank you. With these, we can start charting our own course. Hopefully, without attracting too much attention from Ashbourne or his cronies.
This isn't just any folk tale. The Golden Trout is real, rare, and valuable.
This isn’t about me, we all work together. Remember that, and you can be part of the squad.
This isn’t what I was expecting, but… maybe it will help.
This kind of information leak could ruin the whole Burning Stove operation in the Lowlands.
This lodge will provide a place to sleep along with interiors and a small garden.
This might surprise you, but the Lowlands fishing industry is in a difficult spot at the moment, thanks to those bastards from the Fishing Guild.
This must be a joke, you’re treating him with tea?

This new guy thought he was so clever, joining us and setting us up for the Brigand’s trap. Pathetic. Oh how much fun we had toying with him instead. It’s just a shame that the rat ran away. But we’ll get him. He’s bound to come back for our loot.
This place has a way of robbing you of all hope…
This shack will provide a personal place to sleep for you along with a private chest.
This shack will provide a place to sleep, along with access to a crafting workbench and some basic recipes.
This shared storage container has a significantly increased storage capacity.
This sounds like it could be a disaster. I’m in.
This specialized satchel offers extra food storage for you and your companions, essential for long crusades.
This structure is used for creating cob, a material composed of mud, water and straw - an essential material in building more advanced structures.
This sword was made by a long-gone master swordsmith, the best in the land, he was. I’d love to add it to my collection. I’ll pay you well for it.
This tent will provide shelter for your settlers. Can only accommodate 2 people, so build enough for everyone that might need it.
This time, nothing will stay in our way. We will build a belltower to safeguard Haerndean.

This upgrade from the Lumberjack allows more assigned workers, has a larger storage capacity, and can harvest resources from an wider radius. It also allows the production of planks from various wood.
This upgrade from the Tavern allows more settlers to cook and eat in it, and significantly affects morale.

This village has so much potential, and I truly believe that with the right vision and actions, we can create a brighter future for all its residents.

This village is close to my heart. I was born here and I will likely die here. My ancestors, the oldest in the Lowlands, chose this land as their home. Despite modest means, we have found contentment in working the land with simplicity.
This village is lucky to have you.
This village is lucky to have you. This will not only improve the overall health of the villagers, but yours too if you ever need medical help.
This whole caravan plan could really help the village, I should help the {QuestGiver} with this endeavor.
This’ll really help the trip go smoothly. Thank you, you’ve been a great help to my friends and I.
This… is concerning. I don’t know what to deal with first, the mole or the kidnapped crew.
Those Brigands don't stand a chance

Those Red Thorns, dirty bandits. Took that smuggler caravan right out from under us. Getting the mole inside was expensive, now Ashbourne is going to be mad. But we can fix it if we find the hostages they took.
Those are some terribly serious accusations you’re throwing around. Do you have any proof?
Those blasted Brigands happened! Gods, how I despise them. They came to me in the evening and wanted me to fix their shields.
Those damn Brigands came to me and “asked” me to fix their shields for them. I’m a damn carpenter, not the blacksmith.
Those folks sniffing around Oscar, I’m sure they’re from the Fisher's Guild, mark my words.
Those materials are worth their weight in gold to the right hands. Hide's tough and durable, perfect for shoes or armor. Fur and pelts?
Those outlaws aren’t ordering weapons just to harass us villagers, so it is a win-win situation for the Lowlands.
Those poor Brigands! I hope those spanners can talk things out quickly, so they can get back to make our lives hell! They don’t deserve to be bullied, those fine young men!
Though I heard that the merchant was involved in smuggling books from other regions of Karvenia to the Lowlands. So he was probably on the Lord’s shit-list.

Thought that someone could poison the well deliberately is sickening to me, I wonder who could do such a thing. I better help solve this, hunger is enough of a problem for the Lowlands we don’t need a drinking water shortage.
Threat - Upon attacking brigands your relation will worsen increasing the strength of their forces on the map.
Three axes and two bows should be enough.
Three days ago. Saw the merchant and his cart heading down the road towards Haerndean.
Throwing a feast in these hard times? Seems a bit lavish, doesn't it?
Time for me to go. Let me know if you need any more help.
Times are tough, but we're getting by. Between the Scourge and the Queen's taxes, there's never a dull moment.
Times are tough, but we're getting by. Between the Scourge and the Queen's taxes, there's never a dull moment.
Times are tough, but with a little coin and some savvy trading, one can still thrive.

To all tax-payers of the Lowlands,<cf><cf>Her Highness Queen Osenne has degreed a new tax rate of 45% for all Karvenian serfs, replacing the previous tax rate of 30%. This new tax rate is in effect immediately, and all serfs will have 30 days to submit their first taxation at the updated rate, which must be given to the tax collector assigned to your locality.<cf><cf>Failure to pay the correct amount of tax is punishable by fines, imprisonment and, in the case of repeat offenses, death.<cf><cf>Signed,<cf>Lord Ashbourne.

To all tax-payers of the Lowlands,<cf><cf>Lord Ashbourne, in his noble wisdom, calls for a modest increase in contributions to the community. Fear not, for these coins shall blossom into citadels, walls, and bridges, fortifying our realm against adversity. Rejoice, as prosperity dawns under the benevolent gaze of the Lord of the Lowlands!<cf><cf>Signed,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourn.
To be honest, those spices won’t do much for the flavor. Actually, if you prepare these spices in the right way, they’ll blow your head off from the heat!
To delve deeper, I suggest you visit an old colleague of mine. Despite his eccentricities, his insights into ancient Lowland culture are invaluable.
To find them, look near the lake. They thrive there, spending their days in the mud.
To further our understanding, I suggest consulting an old acquaintance of mine. He's a sage in ancient lore and could offer invaluable insights.
To say at least… And Lord Ashbourne, who rules these lands, come closer, it's safe to say that opinions on him aren't much better. It's a trying time, to be sure.
To say the least. And Lord Aotharm, who rules these lands, well, opinions on him aren't much better. It's a trying time, to be sure.

To the Business Owner,<cf><cf>It has come to our attention that you are stocking and selling copies of a book that has been officially banned by the order of Queen Osenne. The book in question is called The Heroes of the Rebellion, and has been made forbidden in Karvenia due to its pro-rebellion propaganda and anti-monarchy rhetoric.<cf><cf>You have 48 hours to remove the book from your stock, or we will send guards to remove it.<cf><cf>Yours with kindness,<cf>Lord A. Paltar III, Royal Advisor

To the Golden Child of the Lowlands,<cf><cf>Congratulations on liberating so many villages, and releasing the peasants from their perceived shackles! Truly an incredible achievement. I hope it was worth it.<cf><cf>It crossed my mind recently how truly absurd it is that we have never met. Me, as the Lord of the Lowlands, and you, its brave, new savior.<cf><cf>So I would like to cordially invite you to a luncheon in my castle to discuss the future of the Lowlands, as well as the ongoing problems being caused by those damn bandit groups.<cf><cf>I look forward to finally meeting you.<cf><cf>Yours Royally,<cf><cf>Lord Edmund Ashbourne III.
Tomas, I'm so sorry. I found Lyanna in the bandit camp. But she didn't make it.
Too easy, in fact… Can I ask what exactly is heroic about this?
Too many. People I used to consider friends have turned to crime just to provide for their families. It’s truly tragic.
Took you long enough. Do you have it on you?
Traveler, I’m starting to get desperate now…
Traveler, could you help me? My husband is still missing.
Traveler, do you have a moment? I’m still in need of your aid.
Traveler, have you found some time to help me? I still need a Sturdy Falchion and Sneaky Dagger for my experiments.
Traveller please I still need help with finding my husband…

Trust can't be forced, I see that now. I wish it were different.
Trust is a luxury these days. It's not about you; it's about coverin’ my arse.
Trust me when I say, this is important. It’s not just about ego or pride.
Trust me, I’m better with the forge than with excuses. The tools will be ready by tomorrow, no more delays.
Trust me, you don't want to know what she's like when she gets angry; she could have my head.
Trust my husband to kill the cousin of the person who could save his life…
Try to not speak about this too loudly.
Trying to recreate this armor and gift it to the village Elder, hopin’ he’ll allow me back.

Tusk of the most feared creature of the Karvenia - the demon boar tusks. Every hunter dream to have one in their collection.
Two reasons, the roads are a warzone, and my self-defense skills are even worse than my cooking.

Typical, always trying to put on a show. So, when are you going?
Typical. Can’t let me have nothing, can he?
T’was nothing. What you’re doing, it gives us hope.
