Uh… Hello, my friend. I hope the delivery of the letter went well, I truly appreciate your help. Unfortunately, I don’t have time to chat right now, so…

Under his wise guidance, the villagers constructed beautiful homes and nurtured a peaceful community until someone killed a Padowski. Years have passed, and no one knows who could have done that. After the murder, his son took a place as an Elder. As you can see, it worked very well for the village.
Understood. Just gathering info. No harm meant.
Understood. We’ll be announcing the winner soon.
Unfortunately for me, flour production is my main responsibility, and my mill is broken. And I don’t have the parts needed to fix it. If Ashbourne or his men find out, I’m in trouble.
Unfortunately not, they succeeded with fighting boars invasion, but they stayed stubborn and nothing changed to this day.
Unfortunately not. But I hope my story at least gave you something to work with.
Unfortunately there's not. We are just trying to survive really, but I heard some whispers about group of people who are trying to help others in the Lowlands.

Valiant Huntsmen of Farnworth!<cf><cf>In the shadowed depths of the woodlands, where the echoes of the hunt resound, we stand united under the steadfast guidance of Lord Ashbourne, our noble protector and guardian of Farnworth's honor.<cf>Let our hearts swell with pride as we pay homage to his unyielding valor and unwavering dedication to our cherished Farnworth.<cf><cf>Lord Ashbourne, a paragon of courage and resilience, has led us in countless skirmishes to defend our territories and safeguard our hunting grounds from encroaching threats. His recent triumph in repelling a dire incursion from unnamed adversaries stands as a testament to his strategic acumen and resolute spirit.<cf><cf>Entwined in the tapestry of Farnworth's history, Lord Ashbourne epitomizes the essence of bravery and leadership.<cf><cf>From orchestrating daring hunts to ensuring the prosperity of our community, he remains our unwavering sentinel and beacon of hope.<cf>Let us raise our voices in resounding praise and heartfelt gratitude for Lord Ashbourne, whose valor knows no bounds and whose guidance lights our path through the darkest of times. May his name echo through the ages, a symbol of Farnworth's enduring strength and resilience.<cf><cf>Long live Lord Ashbourne! May his legacy endure as an eternal testament to the indomitable spirit of our noble huntsmen!<cf><cf>In unity,<cf>The Council of Great Lord Ashbourne.

We aim for martial superiority, so you need to show that you can handle real combat. There’s a little bandit camp near the village and we wanted to deal with them and you came.

We appreciate your readiness. However, it's important to understand the gravity of this task. When we spark this rebellion, the stationed garrison will become our enemy. Everyone will be in serious danger.
We are a bunch of vagabonds, wanderers, and brave fighters who get together to better themselves in the only arts that matter, MARTIAL ARTS!
We are a small and young community, and we wanted to make things easier for ourselves. Now we want to change and be free, could you be so kind and to help us as our first Protector?
We are in luck it seems, just give me a tick while I whip up the potion.

We are in need of some basic weaponry to protect the village from animals and bandits, we can’t fully rely on the Brigands. So I need you to bring me a few Bastard Swords, this amount should be enough.

We are in your debt, friend. Without you, liberation wouldn't be possible. We will not give our crops to Ashbourne, that dog! Our rich soil will only be used to solve the hunger crisis in the Lowlands.

We began doing many unjust things, even raiding villages around Aldekerk. I decided I had enough.
We believe they call themselves Ophidian.
We call ourselves Burning Stove… Made up of people in both the Lowlands and Highlands. We try to help those who need it across Karvenia.
We can’t rush important things. We still need to prepare. I will be back when I’m ready.
We can’t take them on by ourselves. Burning Stove doesn’t have enough people, and we’re disorganized without our leader around. I’m not ready for this…
We could try to rescue him ourselves, but it's so dangerous. The prison is heavily guarded. We’re gonna lose people regardless.
We could use few sets of clothes and some pairs of shoes. We need to ensure that they’re in decent condition and can withstand the demanding conditions of the mines.
We could use your help with those helmets and gambesons in our trip. Are you interested?
We did it! You did it! You’ve brought peace to our village.
We did it! You did it! You’ve brought peace to our village.
We don't need their so-called ‘protection’ to make a great catch. But I can’t do it by myself, so will you help me, traveler?
We don’t do that here. We may not have much, but we still have honor. Borrowing is for gamblers and scoundrels. It will not happen in my village.
We got into a fight about this whole rigged election business. He thought I was calling him a cheat, and threw a temper tantrum. He won’t make anything for me till I apologize.
We have a deal as long as you tell me the truth.
We have a plan to improve our weekly fishing haul, but we need more Hard Planks and Nails, could you help us with that?
We have been fortunate with our crops compared to the rest of the Lowlands and would be glad to share for the right price.
We have branches in all the lands, and even one on the ocean, so we are quite a big group. We try to stay away from politics, but we always protect any place that welcomes us.
We have learned through many trials to be wary of outsiders such as yourself. Trust is not handed out here freely, it must be earned.
We have the same origin, but they decided to leave our traditions and chose to work with Lord Copue. You can receive more information from Horndean people.
We have to rebuild everything that we lost last time. Remember that a Belltower, tall and vigilant, will be our eyes on the horizon.
We have to rebuild everything that we lost last time. Remember that a belltower, tall and vigilant, will be our eyes on the horizon.
We haven't received anything from him in a while. I made a deal with him for the good of the village, and now it’s starting to backfire because other villages don’t want to cooperate with us.
We heard you’ve been busy, asking questions about some ancient fishing secrets. You're putting a target on your back, kid.
We helped them, so maybe they’ll help us back.
We keep an eye on Guild’s interest around here. People digging up old secrets can cause ripples. You understand, I’m sure.

We must fortify, not just with stones and timber, but with the hearts and arms of every willing soul. I propose we form a militia, volunteers from our own people who will bear arms to defend their kin and soil.
We need allies to survive. It's not like we’re with the crown because we want to be, we are with them because we need to be.
We need quite a bit to restock. Buying copper is too costly for us.
We need some wooden planks and leather to make tables for the celebration, will you be able to deliver them?
We need someone to protect it, and if someone is gonna protect it, they need some basic armor.
We need to get to Blackridgepool and warn people.
We need to prepare better my friend, we need more time.
We need to talk. I can’t stop thinking about that Bradford election. Everyone I talked to was so enthusiastic about Hans Atwood’s plans, but then he loses?
We refused the order to decimate a village, and we got decimated instead. Only I was able to get away. So a lesson for you, kid: think twice before you choose sides.
We should end this conversation now. For your own good.

We're fortunate to have Lord Ashbourne overseeing things. His leadership keeps us all in line, working together for the good of the village. It's that unity that's kept Crasmere prospering all these years.
We're not taking it lightly, that's for sure.
We're short on tools, and our blacksmith is tied up with urgent repairs. Could you bring me some pickaxes and hammers?

We've been granted permission to be here by the Lord. If you have any concerns, I suggest you take them up with him directly. Any aggression towards us will only reflect poorly on your relations with him. I'm sure that's not what you want.
We've been seeing more bandits near the outskirts lately. Everyone's on edge, trying to protect their homes and the little we've harvested.
We've had to deal with the Lord’s heavy taxes too, sending them large portions of our harvest or facing threats from the Brigands.

We've tried, but the bandits are cunning and well-armed. It's hard to stand up to them when they outnumber us and have the upper hand. We're just simple folk, not warriors, and the Lord's people are doing nothing. Most of the attacks happen on the roads, and the bandits have settled in camps all over this land.
We've won! We are finally free!
We? No, we are just here to rest, have a little drink. We are just normal travelers. Just like you.
Weak attempts to discredit my character. In this political climate, everyone is hiding a knife behind their backs.

Weaving's been my life's work. There's a certain peace in it.

Well I am quite old and this wound on my head doesn't help, so it happens often. I am lucky my wife is very patient. I tried my best to reply to her kindness. Remember to have a happy wife and a happy life.
Well at least we got this bag from them. I’ll still pay you.
Well luckily for you and your gods, I am here to learn everything.
Well mate, you showed me that I can trust you, so I have one last wee task for ye.

Well then your friend must have done something to deserve it!

Well, Crude Stone's what you want for the heavy lifting—foundations, walls, even road paving if you're ambitious.

Well, Frank and Andrea were resourceful. They collaborated with a neighboring village skilled in crafting bows and arrows. By working with Padstow, they were able to create a thriving hunting community. As you can imagine, Lowlands is full of interesting stories about beginnings.
Well, I can tell you where the stash is hidden from the convoy raid.
Well, I don’t like to tell this story, but maybe it’ll prevent you from making the same mistakes, so listen up, kid.
Well, I got into a bit of a scrap with some folks from the Fisher's Guild. They had the parts of the rod.
Well, I have to think about it.
Well, I must admit, you've exceeded my expectations. Perhaps there is more to you than idle boasts. Your efforts today will be recognized and rewarded young one.
Well, I must say, your work speaks louder than your words. These are top-notch tools. It seems you have quite the knack for this.
Well, I realized I had dropped my purse while traveling back from the fertilizer merchant. I left the carriage on the side of the road to retrace my steps.
Well, I recorded people’s votes. Or at least, I did that for a half a day. I took one shift collecting votes, while Gilroy Loxley took the other shift.
Well, I'll be! You're a tough one, aren't ya? Done good, real good. Them wolves had it comin'.
Well, I'm mostly dependent on the charity of others. Life's been a struggle, but I try to keep my spirits up. Would you be able to spare some change to help me get by?
Well, I've had my fair share of success with the rod and reel, especially in my younger days. But these days, I'm more concerned with keeping this village together.

Well, I’m not proud of this, but I recently borrowed money from a merchant in the village. Lending money is not a service the merchant normally provides, but I was in a dire situation since my husband died.
Well, aren’t you quite the reliable accomplice? I don’t forget those who help me. We’ll meet again in the future, I’m sure.
Well, at least your men are unharmed.
Well, at the very least, I can buy some good mead for the Solstice with this money. But I’m not sure I can trust you again.
Well, before bandits and brigands ruled the roads of the Lowlands, we were able to trade our wood with other villages to keep everyone fed.
Well, between you and me, things aren't great. The villagers are struggling with hunger, and the Brigands are making life difficult for everyone.
Well, deal’s a deal. Here's the letter with the order.
Well, do you need anything else?
Well, every piece of information is a piece of a larger puzzle.
Well, flax seeds can give you oil for your lamps, but its fibers? They spin into the finest linen for your table or your back.
Well, he mentioned a customer that came to him a few moons ago, trying to offload a rather fine cloak.
Well, he wasn’t the most chatty type, but Gods he is paying a barnload of gold.
Well, here's the thing. I need new tools, but the blacksmith can’t help, and it’s risky getting them from elsewhere.
Well, here’s hoping those other guild branch leaders are little less mental.
Well, he’s a fisherman by trade. So he is often on the water for weeks at a time.
Well, if I ever see her again, I’ll make sure to have a word with her!
Well, if I happen to come across these shrines, I’ll have a look. But it’s really not a priority right now.
Well, if I have to be honest, your methods do sound quite extreme…

Well, if it ain't the hero come at dawn. Too late for poor Bessie, she didn't last the night. Them cursed wolves! They're a menace, a real plague on us all. Someone's gotta do something. You got the guts to take 'em on?
Well, if it rattles the Guild and helps you out, I’m in. I’ll find out about that bait for you.
Well, if there's anything I can do to assist, please let me know. Even if it's just keeping an eye out for trouble.
Well, if there's anything else I can do to assist, please don't hesitate to ask.
Well, if you do want me to find them, just say the word.
Well, if you had the pleasure of meeting her, you’ll know how off-putting she is. I’ve… co-operated with them before, but it didn’t go well, all because of Klara.
Well, it’s a short tale about how you can’t trust no one around here! Except you, of course.
Well, mighty helper of the Lowlands, I have one thing in mind perfectly suited to your skillset.
Well, seeds are your future meals, aren't they? Plant them in well-tended soil, give 'em water and light, and they'll feed you or your livestock.
Well, someone ought to take charge. The villagers need some reassurance right now.
Well, thanks to your efforts, there's been a renewed sense of hope and prosperity. People are working together, and we're seeing improvements in every aspect of village life.
Well, that’s where the story gets even stranger. A few nights after I bought the cloak, two Brigands came in asking about it.
Well, the Brigands are sniffing around the village. And the fact that our Elder wants to cooperate with them is not helping - cowardly old twat.
Well, the Elder heard about it, and wasn’t too pleased. He forbid me from taking the loan, because he thinks it will cause issues in the village. So will you speak to him for me?
Well, the group we most recently rescued are still recovering. And I’m sorry, but I can’t have you badgering them now.
Well, their reasoning is ridiculous, as usual…

Well, there isn't much to say about our village, to be honest. It's still quite young, you see. We came here in search of a new place to call home and settled on this area because of its abundant water resources.
Well, there's been some commotion about a mysterious figure seen at the edge of the forest. Some say it's a wandering mage, others swear it's a lost spirit.
Well, there's been talk of a peculiar light dancing in the old ruins at night. Some say it's the spirit of an ancient guardian, watching over the land.

Well, there's been talk of a traveling minstrel passing through, spinning tales of distant lands and heroic deeds. Some say he's just a storyteller, but others wonder if his songs hold a deeper truth.
Well, this puts me in a difficult situation. You see, we have to be wary of newcomers in our village.

Well, to be honest, things are rather grim. There are still people who believe that Ashbourne is an honest man. It's hard to understand. I mean, I'm pretending that I believe in him because you never know who might be listening.
Well, we got into a little argument.
Well, well, back already? Did you manage to get the bait from Elder Ansgar?
Well, with the use of more advanced tools, Farnworth thrived. The village's ability to utilize these tools attracted traders to its tavern, fostering economic growth and prosperity.
Well, yes but I know what those spices are…
Well, yes, but we’ve done what we can. Your help is much appreciated.
Well, you didn't hear it from me, but there's been speculation about a hidden treasure buried somewhere in the village.
Well, you know how it goes. The village is abuzz with the same old tales and troubles. But, speaking of troubles, it seems like Bradford's dealing with another bout of disagreements with Farnworth.
Well, you know how it is. Times are tough, but folks are keeping their heads down and making do. Can't say much more than that, I'm afraid.
Well, you've got the old blacksmith, always grumbling about his tools. And then there's the weaver, forever spinning tales along with her threads. Quite the pair, those two.
Well, you've got the old blacksmith, always grumbling about his tools. And then there's the weaver, forever spinning tales along with her threads. Quite the pair, those two.
Well, your signs are up. If that doesn't bring in a flood of visitors, I'm starting to think nothing will.
Well, ‘bout that… Listen, there were some wee complications in production. Y’know times are dire and all that…
Well... do you need anything else?
Well? Did you speak with the Elder?
Well? Have you found a boat for me?

Well? What happened with the killer?

Well… I might be interested. What should I do to prove myself?

Well… In my younger years, I was consumed by the thrill of the hunt, seeking grand trophies. But meeting my dear wife changed me. Now, I devote myself to the welfare of this village, caring for its people as if they were my own kin.
Well… We got into a little discussion about how things should work in the village regarding payment…
Well… it looks like I’ve run out of a few ingredients. Can you help me collect them?
Well… you didn't offer me a place to stay for the night, so I had to take matters into my own hands.
Were you able to bring what I asked for?
Were you able to craft them?
We’ll be having the annual Solstice party soon, and I’ve got an idea for injecting some life into it. It’s usually such a boring affair…
We’ll share the spoils. I can finally leave this godforsaken land, and you’ll get a hefty chunk of gold. What do you say?
We’ll talk about it with you later, but I’d like to ask you about something else.
We’re going to have a diplomatic meeting with Blackridgepool to establish a closer trading link. We promised we’d deliver them quivers and bows, and they would reciprocate with crops.
We’re still building trust, so I can’t tell you more than you need to know.
We’re still building trust, so I can’t tell you more than you need to know.

We’ve heard a whisper or two about it.
We’ve tried. But she has a certain way of doing things that doesn’t really fit with our operation. There’s been a lot of arguments about her, and what she can contribute, believe me.

While Blackridgepool historically known for the quality of its crop yield, it didn’t have the most auspicious of starts. The leaders of the village needed three full months to pick out a name for the settlement.<cf><cf>Initially, the village was meant to be called Blackridge, then Ridgepool, then Blackpool. However, all three names were already in use by villages elsewhere. In their infinite wisdom, the leaders then decided to combine all of the proposed names, and so the village became known as Blackridgepool.<cf><cf>And while many have described this origin story as “dumb” and “probably not true”, it is also seen as one of the most enthralling stories in Karvenia’s history by etymology enthusiasts with very little else going on in their lives.<cf><cf>Today, the village is known as the farming powerhouse of the Lowlands, while also having the most needlessly long village name in the Lowlands.

Your villagers can be assigned to one of <highlight>three roles</> at any given time. A <highlight>worker</> fulfills your crafting and production orders in the settlement. A <highlight>companion</> travels with you and follows your commands. A <highlight>guard</> patrols and defends your settlement from danger.

{QuestGiver} has a personal request for me - a stag horn for a helmet. Looks like an ambitious project. I’m curious if it would be a functional piece of armor or only ceremonial.

{QuestGiver} needs Reinforced gambesons and chainmail helmets for the safety of the “trip” she is organizing. I agree, I like to support a good cause.
{QuestGiver} ordered book delivery but the cart with the book never made it to the village. I wonder what happened to it.
{QuestGiver} told me about the injured miners and asked me to bring him some bandages due to shortages. I decided to help them.

{QuestGiver} was ordered by Ashbourne to craft the chairs for his Solstice feast. But the letter was wrongly sent to his former student Marek. I need to retrieve it along with new tools from Sambor. I don’t wanna know what would happen if he’s not able to make those chairs.
“May your wrath be felt across Haerndean's fields. We offer you all we can.” Sounds like they were scared stiff of their gods.

“To my dear wife and daughter - I want to apologize, the Brigands didn't give me a choice. I need to give them this grain or we will not be able to leave this cursed land. I hope we’ll get the gold we need to travel to a better place.<cf><cf>I love you.”

All oases eventually get burnt by the Sun. When it happens, nomads and some walkers on that oasis migrate to the nearest oasis automatically.
Bid against other nomads to reserve slots for your clan at a specified time on this oasis.

Debt is accumulated when you fail to pay taxes to your King.<cf><cf>Every time you don't pay taxes in full, you will be fined with a fee depending on how much you failed to pay.<cf><cf>Once you reach the maximum tax debt, the King will start siezing goods from your town's storage.

Once bought, you can invest resources in Walker storage to increase levels of individual walkers. Levels define how many modules a walker can have.

Population represents how many villagers you have in your town to harvest and produce resources.<cf><cf>Hire NPCs in the world to grow your population, but missing food or water will make them leave your town.<cf><cf>Better food provides more nutrition and can feed more villagers longer. Water always provides the same value<cf><cf>Food requirement is calculated through the same food value used by your character to satisfy hunger.<cf><cf>Working population consumes more food than loiters, so don't waste it by keeping buildings idle while villagers work there.

The King requires every province of the kingdom to pay taxes or face the consequences.<cf><cf>As your mission to settle on this new land comes directly from the King, you've been granted a tax exemption until your territories grow to a large enough size.<cf><cf>Once your capital becomes a proper town at Tier 2, you will be expected to pay taxes as every Lord in the kingdom.<cf><cf>Taxes depend on the size of your population and get collected regularly, which all can be monitored from your Town Hall.

Town Levies act as guards for your town, defending it against enemy forces and upholding the peace.<cf><cf>Villagers can be turned into levies in their individual overview screen.<cf><cf>Make sure to keep your population fed, otherwise Levies will start dying of hunger!<cf><cf>Levies can be recruited as personal troops or released to continue guarding the town.<cf><cf>They will follow you and act as your personal army, but will continue to consume the town's food and water.
